author: Alejandro1400
body:
#### PR description

Adding debugging logging times for the `copy` function of `conddb`, which can be activated by choosing verbose mode with `-v` when calling the `conddb` command for the copy function.

First, it adds a query time listener for SQLAlchemy, as suggested in the [SQLAlchemy docs](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/21/faq/performance.html?#how-can-i-profile-a-sqlalchemy-powered-application), which shows how long each query took. This is called for the sessions opened from the source DB and destination DB defined at the start of the copy function, logging the query timing, counting how many queries were executed, and summing the total query time.

Additionally, timing was added for `copy_tag` and `copy_payload`, logging the execution time as well as the number of payloads and IOVs copied. This only affects the `conddb` file, specifically the `copy` function, while also adding a function to install the query timers so they can potentially be used by other functions if required.

#### PR validation

To validate this PR, I used a test batch job that creates a local DB to which the copy is going to happen, while connecting to a production DB:

```bash
frontier://PromptProd/cms_conditions
```

```bash
{
  time conddb -vv --yes --db pro copy SiPixelQuality_phase1_2021_v1 --destdb $DEST_DB
} 2>&1 | tee -a $LOGFILE
```

From this, I could check that the output was as intended, with the query timing, the per-function timing, and the final summary:

```text
[2026-06-23 12:22:53,204] DEBUG: Source DB session during Copy Executed 7 queries in a total 0.014785 seconds
[2026-06-23 12:22:53,204] DEBUG: Destination DB session during Copy Executed 7 queries in a total 0.025745 seconds
```

I am including the log file as an example with these debug prints:

[copy_tag_query_time.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29247278/copy_tag_query_time.txt)
title: [DRAFT] Conddb logging debug copy queries
